title: User Acquisition Manager (f/m/d)
required_skills: Minimum 1 year experience in user acquisition within mobile gaming industry
Familiarity with managing marketing campaigns across multiple channels with substantial budgets
Hypothesis-driven approach with KPI optimisation focus
Highly motivated, hands-on, well-organised work approach
bonus_skills: Passion for games, especially mobile games
matched_skills: Analytical skills and KPI tracking experience (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd, Intelligent Power Corporation)
Marketing campaign management experience (Moli Media, Intelligent Power Corporation)
ROI analysis and performance reporting (Intelligent Power Corporation, Moli Media)
missing_skills: User acquisition (UA) experience in mobile gaming — no gaming industry experience
Experience with UA channels: Google UAC, Applovin, Unity Ads, Facebook UA
Knowledge of mobile app tracking/attribution (MMP, SKAN, etc.)
Budget management for performance marketing at scale
core_responsibilities: Manage, implement and execute UA campaigns on Google, Applovin, Unity, Facebook
Analyse and optimise UA campaigns based on internal KPIs
Provide input and data-driven decisions into creative development
Keep up with industry trends and new UA opportunities
recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — mobile gaming user acquisition is a specialist discipline requiring platform-specific knowledge (Google UAC, Applovin, Unity) the candidate lacks entirely
No gaming industry background makes this a non-starter
The general marketing and ROI analytics experience is too distant from performance UA to be competitive
summary: Match score is 5.9 (poor_match). This is a specialist performance marketing role in the mobile gaming sector requiring direct UA campaign management experience on gaming-specific channels (Google UAC, Applovin, Unity, Facebook) and deep gaming industry knowledge. The candidate's marketing background is in B2B events and live-stream e-commerce — a completely different context. No gaming experience, no performance UA platform experience. Do not apply.
